title: Easier command to type
question: What command should people type to use the tool, given 'decision' is easy to misspell?
rationale: Add a short '/decide' alias that points to the original '/decision-tree', so the tool is easy to type and nobody has to spell 'decision'. Chosen over a full rename because an alias leaves the install path, the shared viewer link, the hook and the README untouched (the rename d47 rejected) while keeping both names working. The subcommands (add, view, list, plan, revise) stay as-is — they're already short.

options.label: Add a short '/decide' alias (shim skill); keep '/decision-tree'
options.note: A tiny second skill ('decide') whose SKILL.md holds no logic — it just defers to decision-tree's SKILL.md, which stays the single source of truth. A bare folder symlink wouldn't work: the command name comes from the frontmatter 'name', so both would collide on 'decision-tree' instead of giving a distinct '/decide'.

tradeoffs.note: '/decide' has no 'decision' to misspell — the problem we set out to fix
leaves the install path, the d35 viewer symlink, the Stop hook and the README untouched — nothing d47 froze moves
delete one symlink to undo; nothing else changed
adds a third label (product 'Decision Explorer AI', '/decision-tree', '/decide') — the 'two names' smell d47 disliked, and the SKILL.md text still says '/decision-tree' until genericized
one clean name, no dual-name confusion
the hard-to-spell word is gone entirely
rewrites the install path, the d35 viewer symlink, the hook and the README, and breaks anyone's existing '/decision-tree' habit — this is d47's rejected o47c
'decision' stays hard to spell — leaves the problem unsolved
